body:
## Summary

Every release since at least `v0.283.0` publishes **identical release notes**, and `CHANGELOG.md` has grown to **609,927 lines**. Both symptoms have one root cause: the auto-release path collects changelog fragments but **never deletes them**, so every release re-publishes all 388 fragments in `changelog.d/` — including ones dated December 2025.

Release notes for `v0.283.0` … `v0.289.0` are byte-for-byte identical and describe changelog tooling from months ago. Real user-visible fixes shipped in those releases (e.g. #680, #681, #682) are **invisible** in the release notes, even though correct fragments for them exist in `changelog.d/`.

## Impact

- Release notes are useless — they never describe what actually shipped.
- Users cannot tell which release contains a given fix. (Concretely: #680/#681/#682 were fixed in `v0.284.0`–`v0.286.0`, but nothing in any release note says so.)
- `CHANGELOG.md` grows ~4,600 lines per release and is now ~610k lines / 289 duplicate-content version sections.
- The notes actively misinform: every release announces `get-bump-type.mjs` / `collect-changelog.mjs` as **new**, but those scripts were ported to `.rs` months ago. That text comes from `changelog.d/20251227_224645_changeset_support.md` — accurate when written in Dec 2025, replayed into every release since.

## Root cause

Two scripts implement fragment collection, and the two release paths use different ones:

| Path | Job | Script used | Deletes fragments? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Manual (`workflow_dispatch`) | `manual-release` | `scripts/collect-changelog.rs` | ✅ yes (`fs::remove_file`, line 199) |
| **Automatic (push to main)** | `auto-release` | `scripts/version-and-commit.rs` (inlined copy) | ❌ **no** |

`.github/workflows/release.yml` line ~449, the `auto-release` job:

```yaml
- name: Collect changelog and bump version
  run: rust-script scripts/version-and-commit.rs --bump-type "..."
```

`version-and-commit.rs` has its own `collect_changelog()` (line 346) that reads and concatenates every fragment, but contains **no** `remove_file` / `git rm` — verified:

```console
$ grep -nE "remove_file|fs::remove|git rm" scripts/version-and-commit.rs
(no output)

$ grep -nE "remove_file|fs::remove|git rm" scripts/collect-changelog.rs
199:  if fs::remove_file(&path).is_ok() {
```

Since the automatic path is what runs on merge to main, fragments accumulate forever and are re-collected on every release.

Note the insert logic itself is **fine** — `## [` insertion works and versions land in correct descending order. Deletion is the only missing step.

## Deterministic repro (no CI needed)

```bash
mkdir repro && cd repro
printf '[package]\nname="repro"\nversion="1.0.0"\nedition="2021"\n' > Cargo.toml
printf '# Changelog\n\n<!-- changelog-insert-here -->\n' > CHANGELOG.md
mkdir changelog.d
printf -- '---\nbump: patch\n---\n\n### Fixed\n- FRAGMENT_ALPHA\n' > changelog.d/20260101_alpha.md
printf -- '---\nbump: patch\n---\n\n### Fixed\n- FRAGMENT_BETA\n'  > changelog.d/20260102_beta.md
git init -q . && git add -A && git -c user.email=t@t -c user.name=t commit -qm init

cp /path/to/formal-ai/scripts/version-and-commit.rs .
cp /path/to/formal-ai/scripts/rust-paths.rs .

rust-script version-and-commit.rs --bump-type patch      # 1.0.0 -> 1.0.1
git add -A && git -c user.email=t@t -c user.name=t commit -qm "release 1.0.1"
rust-script version-and-commit.rs --bump-type patch      # 1.0.1 -> 1.0.2, NO new fragments
```

**Actual `CHANGELOG.md`** — 1.0.2 shipped zero new fragments yet re-published both:

```markdown
## [1.0.2] - 2026-07-14

### Fixed
- FRAGMENT_ALPHA

### Fixed
- FRAGMENT_BETA

## [1.0.1] - 2026-07-14

### Fixed
- FRAGMENT_ALPHA

### Fixed
- FRAGMENT_BETA
```

`ls changelog.d/*.md` → still 2 files after both releases.

**Expected:** `1.0.2` contains no entries (nothing new shipped); `changelog.d/` is empty after `1.0.1`.

## Production evidence

```
lines added to CHANGELOG.md per release commit:
  v0.283.0: +4619    v0.287.0: +4681
  v0.284.0: +4622    v0.288.0: +4684
  v0.285.0: +4666    v0.289.0: +4687
  v0.286.0: +4673

$ wc -l CHANGELOG.md            -> 609927
$ grep -c '^## \[' CHANGELOG.md -> 290   (289 distinct versions, near-identical bodies)
$ ls changelog.d/*.md | wc -l   -> 388   (oldest: 20251227_*, never consumed)
```

## Suggested fix

Make the automatic path use the same collector as the manual path, and stage the deletions in the release commit. Either:

1. **Preferred — de-duplicate:** have `auto-release` call `scripts/collect-changelog.rs` (which already deletes correctly) before `version-and-commit.rs`, and drop the inlined `collect_changelog()` from `version-and-commit.rs`. Two implementations of one behavior is what allowed them to drift.
2. **Minimal:** add fragment deletion to `collect_changelog()` in `version-and-commit.rs` after a successful write, ensuring the removals are `git add`-ed into the release commit.

Either way, a regression test asserting *"release N+1 with no new fragments produces an empty/absent section, and `changelog.d/` is empty afterward"* would lock this down.

## Cleanup required

The fix alone won't repair history: `CHANGELOG.md` needs to be regenerated/truncated (289 bogus duplicate sections), and the 388 stale fragments in `changelog.d/` triaged — the genuinely-unreleased ones collected once, the rest deleted.

---

Found while verifying #680/#681/#682 against `v0.289.0`.

## 🚨 Solution Draft Failed

The automated solver stopped before creating a pull request, so no PR was opened for this issue.

### Failure
- **Repository**: `link-assistant/formal-ai`
- **Issue**: #711
- **Tool**: `agent`
- **Requested model**: `formal-ai`

**Reason**
```text
Invalid model name
```

### What you can do
- Resolve the repository, account, permissions, or environment problem described above, then rerun the solver.
- Repository owner or Hive Mind administrator path: handle manual recreation or fix of the repository when the required action is outside the requester access.

Logs were not attached because `--attach-logs` was not enabled.

title: CI/CD: every release republishes all 388 changelog fragments — identical release notes since v0.283.0, CHANGELOG.md at 610k lines
